div.h1: ROTR32 vs SHR32 Bit-Level Trace on 0xABCDE001
div.p: ROTR(x,7) wraps 7 bits; SHR(x,7) destroys them
0000 000|1 0101 0111 1001 1011 1101 1110 positions 31–25 = 0000 000 (vacated by shift) bits 24–0 carry the shifted content
0000 010|0 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 positions 31–25 = 000 0010 (the 7 bits 0000001 wrapped) bit at position 0 is now at position 25
0000 000|1 0101 0111 1001 1011 1101 1110 positions 31–25 = 000 0000 (filled with zeros) 7 bits at 6–0 are permanently GONE

g.text: Why SHA-256 σ functions use BOTH

div.p: σ0(x) = ROTR(x,7) ⊕ ROTR(x,18) ⊕ SHR(x,3) · ROTR terms: Diffusion, zero bit loss. · SHR term: One-way bit destruction. · Security: Prevents invertibility of the schedule.
